nodes.name: RAG Without Citations or Provenance
nodes.description: **RAG Without Citations or Provenance** means answering from retrieved material without showing what was used, so users cannot check whether the answer is grounded or guessed. It belongs in hold for enterprise settings. If the system cannot show where an answer came from, debugging trust failures becomes much harder.

tags.citation: These sampling strategies help you find traces more likely to reveal problems.

nodes.name: One Giant System Prompt for Everything
nodes.description: **One Giant System Prompt for Everything** means stuffing every rule, edge case, and instruction into one huge prompt and hoping it remains understandable and maintainable. It is an attractive nuisance. Current best practice is drifting toward layered context, reusable instruction files, and task-specific harnesses instead of a single sprawling prompt blob.

tags.citation: Almost all forms of AI coding context engineering ultimately involve a bunch of markdown files with prompts.

nodes.name: High-Temperature Generation in Transactional Workflows
nodes.description: **High-Temperature Generation in Transactional Workflows** means letting the model be very random in jobs where precision matters, like forms, SQL, code changes, or money movement. It is usually the wrong default for workflows that send emails, edit data, execute tools, or touch money. These paths need determinism, schema validation, and explicit approvals.

tags.citation: supervisory engineering work - the effort required to direct AI, evaluate its output, and correct it when it's wrong.

nodes.name: Multi-Agent Theater Before Single-Agent Reliability
nodes.description: **Multi-Agent Theater Before Single-Agent Reliability** means building a fancy swarm of agents before you have proved one well-designed agent can do the core job reliably. It belongs in hold when teams add planners, critics, and swarms before they can make a single-agent flow dependable. Several practitioners now treat extra agents as something to earn, not assume.

tags.citation: Most are thin wrappers around Anthropic's SDK + work tree management. There's no moat.

nodes.name: Verifier-Guided Generation
nodes.description: **Verifier-Guided Generation** means pairing generation with a checker that can reject, score, or request another try until the result meets a measurable standard. It is worth assessing wherever correctness can be checked externally. The pattern is especially promising for code, math, structured extraction, and constrained decision points.

tags.citation: A comprehensive guide to LLM evals, drawn from questions asked in our popular course on AI Evals.

nodes.name: Context Distillation / Working-Memory Summaries
nodes.description: **Context Distillation / Working-Memory Summaries** means condensing a long interaction into shorter summaries that keep the important facts active without carrying every old token forward. It is worth assessing as agents take on longer tasks. It sits between stateless sessions and full persistent memory, and can reduce cost and drift if implemented carefully.

tags.citation: They do the context engineering.

nodes.name: Legacy OpenAI Assistants API
nodes.description: **Legacy OpenAI Assistants API** is OpenAI’s older agent-style API abstraction, which has now been superseded by newer interfaces. It now belongs in hold for new work. OpenAI has deprecated it and recommends Responses instead, so new projects should avoid building on the older abstraction.

tags.citation: After achieving feature parity in the Responses API, we've deprecated the Assistants API.

nodes.name: AutoGPT Classic
nodes.description: **AutoGPT Classic** is one of the early open-source autonomous-agent projects that popularized the idea of AI agents planning and acting by themselves. It is historically important but belongs in hold. It was an influential demo of autonomous agents, not the shape of reliable modern production systems.

tags.citation: AutoGPT Classic was an experimental project to demonstrate autonomous GPT-4 operation.

nodes.name: AgentGPT
nodes.description: **AgentGPT** is a browser-based agent product built around giving an autonomous AI a goal and letting it try to pursue it. They belong in hold for serious delivery. They are useful for demos and intuition, but they encourage open-ended autonomy without the harnessing, evals, and controls that production work needs.

tags.citation: Assemble, configure, and deploy autonomous AI Agents in your browser.

nodes.name: Google AI Edge SDK
nodes.description: **Google AI Edge SDK** is Google’s older toolkit for running certain AI features on-device at the edge, especially on mobile. It belongs in hold because Google has already deprecated the experimental path. Teams interested in on-device Gemini should use the newer ML Kit interfaces instead.

alternatives: Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API
tags.citation: Caution: The experimental Google AI Edge SDK is deprecated.

nodes.name: Standalone Prompt Playgrounds as Production Control Planes
nodes.description: **Standalone Prompt Playgrounds as Production Control Planes** means using a prompt playground tool as the main place to manage production behavior, approvals, and changes, instead of a real engineering workflow. They are useful for exploration, but they belong in hold as the primary operating surface for production systems. Serious teams now need versioning, traces, evals, and code review around prompt changes.

tags.citation: Humanloop is the LLM evals platform for teams to ship AI products that succeed.

nodes.name: OCR-First Browser Automation When APIs Exist
nodes.description: **OCR-First Browser Automation When APIs Exist** means reading screens with OCR and clicking around like a human even when proper APIs are available. It belongs in hold when a stable API is available. It is usually slower, more fragile, and less secure than structured integrations.

tags.citation: Tool use The agent learns to call external APIs for extra information that is missing from the model weights

nodes.name: Notebook-Only Prompt Operations
nodes.description: **Notebook-Only Prompt Operations** means running important prompt and model work entirely from ad hoc notebooks without proper versioning, review, or deployment controls. They belong in hold once a system matters. They make it too easy to lose version history, evaluation context, and operational discipline.

tags.citation: supervisory engineering work - the effort required to direct AI, evaluate its output, and correct it when it's wrong.

nodes.name: Prompt-Injection Scanners as a Sole Security Control
nodes.description: **Prompt-Injection Scanners as a Sole Security Control** means treating one scanner or filter as if it can fully protect an AI system from prompt injection attacks. They belong in hold. Scanning may help, but current expert consensus is that this problem is architectural, not just lexical.

alternatives: Red Teaming for Prompt Injection and Tool Abuse
tags.citation: Prompt injection remains an unsolved problem.

nodes.name: Blanket “Dangerous Permissions” Agent Runners
nodes.description: **Blanket “Dangerous Permissions” Agent Runners** means using agent tools that ask for very broad permissions up front and then trust the model not to misuse them. They belong in hold for shared or sensitive environments. They may be acceptable for isolated personal sandboxes, but they are the wrong default for enterprise systems.

tags.citation: I’ve been building an incredible amount of custom infra, admin pages, CLIs to help both me and the agents.

nodes.name: Unsandboxed Web Browsing in Tool-Using Agents
nodes.description: **Unsandboxed Web Browsing in Tool-Using Agents** means letting an agent browse the live web and use the results directly without isolating or constraining that environment. In tool-using agents belongs in hold. Untrusted pages plus powerful tools is exactly the combination that current security writing warns about most strongly.

tags.citation: Prompt injection remains an unsolved problem.

nodes.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
nodes.description: **OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions** is the OpenTelemetry effort to describe AI traces and metrics in a common vocabulary that different tools can share. They are worth assessing now. They look strategically important for reducing vendor lock-in in observability, but the spec is still explicitly in development.

tags.citation: Semantic conventions for generative AI systems. Status: Development.

nodes.name: Humanloop
nodes.description: **Humanloop** is a platform for prompt management, human review, and evaluation workflows around AI products. It is worth assessing for teams that want prompt management, evaluations, and observability in one workflow. It fits the expert push toward disciplined iteration, though many teams will still prefer code-first stacks.

tags.citation: Humanloop is the LLM evals platform for teams to ship AI products that succeed.

nodes.name: Consumer Chat Apps as Production Integration Layers
nodes.description: **Consumer Chat Apps as Production Integration Layers** means building important business workflows on top of consumer chat products instead of a controlled application stack you own. They belong in hold as the core platform layer for enterprise automations. They are excellent end-user surfaces, but the control plane, permissions, and audit model should usually live elsewhere.

tags.citation: A harness is a system that lets the AI use tools, take actions, and complete multi-step tasks on its own.

nodes.name: Single-Provider Inference Without Routing or Fallback
nodes.description: **Single-Provider Inference Without Routing or Fallback** means depending on one model provider with no backup path if quality, price, rate limits, or outages change. It belongs in hold for workloads that matter. Model quality, price, rate limits, and outages change too quickly for hard-wired monocultures to feel like a durable architecture.

tags.citation: A harness is a system that lets the AI use tools, take actions, and complete multi-step tasks on its own.

nodes.name: Preview / Experimental Agent Platforms as Core Infrastructure
nodes.description: **Preview / Experimental Agent Platforms as Core Infrastructure** means treating still-changing preview agent products as the foundation of production systems. They belong in hold as core infrastructure. They are fine for learning and prototypes, but too much of the current agent stack still ships with changing semantics and immature operational guarantees.

tags.citation: A harness is a system that lets the AI use tools, take actions, and complete multi-step tasks on its own.

nodes.name: Blanket Workspace Connectors for Agents
nodes.description: **Blanket Workspace Connectors for Agents** means giving agents wide access to whole document, email, or chat systems instead of carefully scoped data sources. They belong in hold when agents get sweeping access to mail, docs, drives, tickets, and chat by default. Least privilege should be the baseline, not the retrofit.

tags.citation: Prompt injection remains an unsolved problem.

nodes.name: Public MCP Servers on Sensitive Data
nodes.description: **Public MCP Servers on Sensitive Data** means exposing important internal tools or data through MCP endpoints that are too open for the risk level. They belong in hold for sensitive data paths. MCP is useful, but Simon Willison’s security critique is exactly why mature teams should assume hostile inputs and narrow permissions aggressively.

tags.citation: Prompt injection remains an unsolved problem.

nodes.name: Always-On Desktop Agents with Broad OS Permissions
nodes.description: **Always-On Desktop Agents with Broad OS Permissions** are agents that stay active on a user’s machine and can broadly access files, apps, and system controls. That can feel magical for an individual power user, but for most organizations it creates a very large security and privacy risk, which is why it belongs in hold.

tags.citation: Prompt injection remains an unsolved problem.

nodes.name: Cloud-Only Architectures for Privacy-Sensitive On-Device Use Cases
nodes.description: **Cloud-Only Architectures for Privacy-Sensitive On-Device Use Cases** means sending everything to the cloud even when the product would be safer, faster, or more private on-device. It belongs in hold for some privacy-sensitive mobile and edge use cases. On-device models are still constrained, but the platform story is improving enough that ignoring them entirely is now a mistake.

alternatives: Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API
tags.citation: The Foundation Models framework provides access to Apple's on-device large language model.

nodes.name: Front-End Apps Calling Frontier Models Without a Controlled Backend
nodes.description: **Front-End Apps Calling Frontier Models Without a Controlled Backend** means browser or mobile apps talk directly to powerful model APIs without a server layer to enforce policy, logging, or key management. They belong in hold for serious systems unless carefully brokered. Teams usually need a backend for policy enforcement, secret handling, logging, and cost controls.

tags.citation: supervisory engineering work - the effort required to direct AI, evaluate its output, and correct it when it's wrong.

nodes.name: Improvised Self-Hosting of Frontier Weights
nodes.description: **Improvised Self-Hosting of Frontier Weights** means trying to run huge cutting-edge models yourself without the operational maturity, security, and hardware planning that serious hosting requires. Of large frontier-class weights belongs in hold unless you have a strong platform team. The operational burden around serving, batching, security, upgrades, and cost can be worse than teams expect.

tags.citation: I’ve been building an incredible amount of custom infra, admin pages, CLIs to help both me and the agents.

nodes.name: Browser UI Automation Platforms for Core Back-Office Workflows
nodes.description: **Browser UI Automation Platforms for Core Back-Office Workflows** means relying on screen-scraping and browser clicks as the main integration method for important internal systems. They belong in hold as the primary backbone for back-office workflows when APIs exist. Keep them as a last resort or compatibility layer, not the preferred platform choice.

tags.citation: An agent is anything that can perceive its environment and act upon that environment.

nodes.name: Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)
nodes.description: **Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)** is an emerging protocol meant to let one AI agent talk to another in a more standard way. It is worth assessing as a serious attempt at cross-agent interoperability. The direction makes sense, but the ecosystem is young enough that teams should prototype before standardizing on it.

tags.citation: A2A provides the definitive common language for agent interoperability.

nodes.name: Apple Foundation Models
nodes.description: **Apple Foundation Models** is Apple’s on-device and platform-level model offering for building AI features into Apple apps. They are worth assessing for privacy-first, offline, and Apple-centric experiences. They are strategically important, but still narrower in reach than cloud-hosted general-purpose platforms.

alternatives: Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API
tags.citation: The Foundation Models framework provides access to Apple's on-device large language model.

nodes.name: Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API
nodes.description: **Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API** is Google’s mobile path for running Gemini Nano features on supported devices through ML Kit APIs. It is worth assessing for Android-native, on-device features. It is especially compelling when low latency, privacy, and intermittent connectivity matter more than frontier breadth.

tags.citation: With ML Kit's GenAI Prompt API, you can send natural language requests on-device to Gemini Nano.

nodes.name: Letta
nodes.description: **Letta** is a framework for stateful agents that puts memory management front and center. It is worth assessing if you believe stateful agents and explicit memory management will become a first-class product capability. It is pushing an important frontier, but the design trade-offs around persistence and privacy are still substantial.

tags.citation: In Letta, all state, includes memories, user messages, reasoning, tool calls, are all persisted in a database.

nodes.name: Semantic Router
nodes.description: **Semantic Router** is a lightweight framework for routing requests by meaning, such as deciding which prompt, tool, or model should handle an input. It is worth assessing for deterministic dispatch, safety routing, and model selection. It reflects a broader move away from using full LLM generations for every routing decision.

tags.citation: Deterministic Decision Making for AI.

nodes.name: Transformers.js
nodes.description: **Transformers.js** is the JavaScript version of the Transformers ecosystem, letting developers run some models in the browser or Node.js. It is worth assessing for browser-native and privacy-preserving GenAI workloads. WebGPU makes it more viable than it used to be, but performance and model limits still depend heavily on the client environment.

alternatives: Gemini Nano via ML Kit Prompt API
tags.citation: enabling WebGPU acceleration is as simple as setting `device: 'webgpu'`

nodes.name: OpenAI Swarm
nodes.description: **OpenAI Swarm** is a small experimental framework from OpenAI for coordinating multiple agents, mainly as an educational example. It belongs in hold for production choices because even OpenAI presents it as an educational exploration. It is useful for learning, not as a durable recommendation for core systems.

tags.citation: Educational framework exploring ergonomic, lightweight multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: BabyAGI-Style Self-Building Agent Frameworks
nodes.description: **BabyAGI-Style Self-Building Agent Frameworks** are frameworks built around the idea that agents can recursively plan, create, and manage their own tasks with minimal human direction. They belong in hold for most serious work. They remain influential as research toys, but they blur execution, memory, and governance in ways that production teams struggle to bound.

tags.citation: Not meant for production use. Use with cautioun.

nodes.name: MetaGPT
nodes.description: **MetaGPT** is a multi-agent framework that models software development as a team of specialized agent roles. It captures the appeal of role-playing software-company agents, but it belongs in hold as a default architectural choice. Most teams need dependable task decomposition and review loops more than simulated org charts.

tags.citation: Assign different roles to GPTs to form a collaborative software entity for complex tasks.

nodes.name: Framework-First Multi-Agent Architectures
nodes.description: **Framework-First Multi-Agent Architectures** means choosing a complex agent framework and role system before proving that the product really needs it. It belongs in hold when the architecture is chosen before the failure modes are understood. The newer expert writing is consistently pushing teams to master one good loop before adding a society of agents.

tags.citation: Most are thin wrappers around Anthropic's SDK + work tree management. There's no moat.

nodes.name: YAML-Only Agent DSLs
nodes.description: **YAML-Only Agent DSLs** means defining agent behavior mostly in configuration files instead of clear, testable application code. They belong in hold when they replace readable code and tests. They can be convenient for demos, but they often become opaque once logic, validation, and debugging get real.

tags.citation: Almost all forms of AI coding context engineering ultimately involve a bunch of markdown files with prompts.

nodes.name: Recursive Self-Improving Agent Frameworks
nodes.description: **Recursive Self-Improving Agent Frameworks** are systems built on the idea that agents can repeatedly redesign or improve themselves with little outside control. They belong in hold. They are intellectually exciting, but the current production conversation is moving toward bounded iteration, explicit evals, and narrow autonomy rather than open-ended self-rewrite.

tags.citation: A comprehensive guide to LLM evals, drawn from questions asked in our popular course on AI Evals.

nodes.name: Framework-Managed Memory Without Explicit Deletion and Review
nodes.description: **Framework-Managed Memory Without Explicit Deletion and Review** means letting a framework store and reuse long-term memory automatically without clear controls for cleanup, privacy, or correction. It belongs in hold when it accumulates quietly without lifecycle controls. Memory can help, but persistent state needs reviewability, deletion, and scoping built in.

tags.citation: In Letta, all state, includes memories, user messages, reasoning, tool calls, are all persisted in a database.

nodes.name: Natural-Language-Only Tool Contracts
nodes.description: **Natural-Language-Only Tool Contracts** means tools are described only in prose instead of with strongly typed input and output rules that software can validate. They belong in hold. Modern agent systems are getting more robust by making tool interfaces explicit, typed, and testable rather than implicit in prompt text.

tags.citation: Almost all forms of AI coding context engineering ultimately involve a bunch of markdown files with prompts.

nodes.name: Regex / XML Parsers Instead of Native Structured Outputs
nodes.description: **Regex / XML Parsers Instead of Native Structured Outputs** means forcing model output through brittle text parsing even when the model or API can return typed data directly. They belong in hold as the default integration pattern for LLM outputs. Native schema-constrained generation has moved far enough that brittle text scraping should now be the exception.

tags.citation: Responses is recommended for all new projects.

nodes.name: Low-Code Agent Builders as Core Engineering Platforms
nodes.description: **Low-Code Agent Builders as Core Engineering Platforms** means making a drag-and-drop agent builder the main foundation for serious engineering work instead of a thinner layer above code. They belong in hold as the core engineering platform for complex systems. They can speed up prototypes, but the experts driving current best practice keep returning to code-first harnesses, explicit tests, and custom control.

tags.citation: I’ve been building an incredible amount of custom infra, admin pages, CLIs to help both me and the agents.
